FERS Retirement

Overview The Formula Eligibility FERS Supplement Military Buyback Sick Leave Chart FAQ
FERS Retirement

Maximize Your Federal

Pension.

Your FERS pension forms the foundation of your federal retirement. Understanding your annuity calculation, retirement timing, and benefit coordination requires specialized expertise to maximize lifetime value. Start with our free FERS calculator to see where you stand.

Calculate My FERS

"Your FERS pension could be worth nearly $1 million over your retirement."

Mike Stevens
Also Seen On
FERS Formula
Service × High-3 × Multiplier
Standard Multiplier
1.0% (1.1% enhanced)
MRA + 30 Years
Full pension + supplement
COLA Protection
Full inflation adjustment
Military Buyback
3% of base pay + interest

FERS Foundation Components

Your Federal Employees Retirement System includes multiple benefits that must work together for optimal retirement income coordination.

Annuity Calculation

Master the formula: years of service × high-3 salary × multiplier. Understand when you qualify for the enhanced 1.1% multiplier and how special category employees get 1.7%.

Retirement Eligibility

Multiple pathways to retirement with different requirements and benefits. Know your MRA, understand immediate versus deferred retirement, and optimize your timing.

FERS Supplement

Bridge benefit from retirement until Social Security eligibility at 62. Understand who qualifies, earnings test limits, and coordination strategies.

Survivor Benefits

Critical irrevocable decision on spouse protection. Compare full survivor benefit, partial benefits, and no election to optimize lifetime income.

The Foundation

Your FERS Investment.

Pension Value

Lifetime Benefits

Average FERS Annuity $1,834/mo
30-Year Career Value $650Klifetime
Employee Contribution 0.8%of salary

Your FERS pension represents enormous value — often worth more than your TSP balance. Small optimizations in timing and coordination can add tens of thousands in lifetime benefits.

Optimization Areas

Maximize Your Benefits

Strategic FERS planning coordinates multiple moving pieces to optimize your lifetime benefits through careful timing and election decisions.

  • High-3 salary timing
  • Retirement date optimization
  • Military service buyback analysis
  • Sick leave credit maximization
  • Survivor benefit evaluation
  • FERS supplement coordination
  • Social Security timing
  • Tax minimization strategies
Free Tool

Calculate your FERS pension

Estimate your federal pension using your high-3 salary, years of service, sick leave, and retirement age. Includes the 1.1% enhanced multiplier and LEO/Fire calculations.

Try the FERS calculator →
Strategic Planning

Beyond the
Basic Formula

While the FERS annuity formula appears simple, optimizing your benefits requires understanding eligibility nuances, timing strategies, and coordination with other federal benefits and outside assets.

Military Service Credit

Buying back military time can add years to your service calculation. We analyze the cost versus lifetime benefit to determine if the 3% deposit makes financial sense for your situation.

Sick Leave Conversion

Unused sick leave converts to additional service credit at retirement. Strategic leave management in your final years can boost your annuity calculation and lifetime benefits.

Reference

Sick Leave Conversion Chart

See how your unused sick leave hours convert to additional months and days of creditable service under FERS. Find your sick leave balance in the left column, then read across to find the added service credit.

Hours 0 Days 1 Day 2 Days 3 Days 4 Days 5 Days 6 Days 7 Days 8 Days 9 Days 10 Days 11 Days 12 Days 13 Days 14 Days 15 Days 16 Days 17 Days 18 Days 19 Days 20 Days 21 Days 22 Days 23 Days 24 Days 25 Days 26 Days 27 Days 28 Days 29 Days
0 Mo0612172329354146525864707581879399104110116122128133139145151157162168
1 Mo174180186191197203209215220226232238244249255261267273278284290296302307313319325331336342
2 Mo348354360365371377383389394400406412418423429435441447452458464470476481487493499505510516
3 Mo522528534539545551557563568574580586592597603609615621626632638644650655661667673679684690
4 Mo696702708713719725731737742748754760766771777783789795800806812818824829835841847853858864
5 Mo8708768828878938999059119169229289349409459519579639699749809869929981003100910151021102710321038
6 Mo104410501056106110671073107910851090109611021108111411191125113111371143114811541160116611721177118311891195120112061212
7 Mo121812241230123512411247125312591264127012761282128812931299130513111317132213281334134013461351135713631369137513801386
8 Mo139213981404140914151421142714331438144414501456146214671473147914851491149615021508151415201525153115371543154915541560
9 Mo156615721578158315891595160116071612161816241630163616411647165316591665167016761682168816941699170517111717172317281734
10 Mo174017461752175717631769177517811786179217981804181018151821182718331839184418501856186218681873187918851891189719021908
11 Mo191419201926193119371943194919551960196619721978198419891995200120072013201820242030203620422047205320592065207120762082

Based on a 2,087-hour work year. 1 month = 174 hours, 1 day ≈ 5.797 hours. Source: OPM FERS guidelines.

How to Read This Chart

Find the row matching your full months of credit, then read across to the column for remaining days. The number in the cell is the total sick leave hours needed. For example, to earn 3 months and 10 days of additional service credit, you need 580 hours of unused sick leave.

Why Sick Leave Matters

Under FERS, unused sick leave at retirement is converted to creditable service for annuity computation. This can meaningfully boost your pension. If you have 1,044 hours (6 months) of sick leave, that adds half a year of service to your annuity calculation.

Need Help Calculating?

Your sick leave balance, combined with your years of service and high-3 salary, determines your total pension. Schedule a consultation for a personalized FERS benefit analysis.
